channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Next Iran-US nuclear talks will be held in Rome, AP source says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program appeared ready Monday to leave the Middle East, as an Italian source said the next round of negotiations would take place in Rome.
item.dc:creator: MARIA GRAZIA MURRU and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rebellion trial opens for South Korea's ousted president. Here's what to know
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol's legal saga is far from over. Ten days after he was ousted from office over his martial law declaration, the former conservative South Korean president appeared for the first time at his criminal trial Monday on high-stakes rebellion charges.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 4 passengers from capsized boat off Florida coast; 1 dead, 5 still missing
item.description: A search for five missing passengers who were on a boat that capsized off the coast of Florida remains active after authorities rescued four passengers and found one dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect arrested in arson fire that forced Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro, family to flee residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion where he set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAA says company whose sightseeing chopper crashed, killing 6, is ceasing operations immediately
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Federal Aviation Administration said Sunday that the helicopter tour company whose sightseeing chopper broke apart in flight and crashed in New York, killing the pilot and a family of five visitors from Spain, is shutting down operations immediately.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, WGN 9

item.title: A beloved pet tortoise is reunited with its family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: KOKOMO, Miss. (AP) — Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March. “He’s been through a lot,” said Myrtle's grateful owner, Tiffany Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania police arrest suspect after governor’s mansion set on fire while Gov Josh Shapiro, family slept
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and his family are safe after authorities say an arsonist set fire to the governor's residence while they were asleep. Cody Balmer, 38, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on how flat funding set stage for Texas measles outbreak, might fuel more
item.description: The measles outbreak in West Texas didn’t happen just by chance. The easily preventable disease, declared eliminated in the U.S. in 2000, ripped through Texas communities in part because health departments were starved of the funding needed to run vaccine programs, officials say.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR, MICHELLE R. SMITH and DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Arizona recreation area closed after hiker death and nearly 3 dozen rescues in just 2 days
item.description: Arizona’s Lake Mead National Recreation Area have closed multiple trails due to dangerously high temperatures and the death of one hiker and rescue of 33 others over two days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utahns express concerns over potential impacts of 'SAVE Act' on women voters
item.description: A bill under consideration in Washington could change the way you register to vote, and critics believe it could disenfranchise women at the polls.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?”
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: South Side nonprofit Project HOOD rings New York Stock Exchange opening bell
item.description: Project HOOD provides educational resources and tutoring to thousands of students, helping them achieve academic success.
item.dc:creator: Micah Materre, WGN 9

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge on Friday lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minecraft 'Chicken Jockey' trend hits Utah theaters, movie-goer kicked out after bringing live chicken
item.description: Videos online show crowds watching 'A Minecraft Movie' in theaters cheering wildly as part of a viral trend — but, after a recent event with streamers, air horns, and a live chicken in a Utah theater, some are saying the trend has gone too far.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schools lined up for help getting cleaner school buses. Then came the EPA freeze
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Looking to cut pollution from its fleet of diesel-burning school buses, an Oklahoma school district last year doubled its electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon’s network includes millions of third-party sellers who are either in China or whose products originate from there.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey. The crash comes at a time of heightened scrutiny of aircraft collisions and near-misses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Grand jury indicts man for threats against Augusta National employee
item.description: A Georgia grand jury has returned an indictment against a man for his role in making threats against an employee of the Augusta National Golf Club.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, Fox 5

item.title: US stocks dive as euphoria on Wall Street reverts to fear about US-China trade war
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks dove Thursday and surrendered a chunk of their historic gains from the day before as President Donald Trump’s trade war continues to threaten the economy.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Johnson presses ahead with vote on Trump budget blueprint
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said he was pressing forward with a vote Thursday morning on the party's “one big, beautiful bill” full of President Trump’s legislative agenda, holding a joint appearance with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9
